project_summary: This project is to capture the main requirements and constraints for the 1999_dii document, based on the 1999 - dii system.

propositions.text: The system shall provide XS services to several different classes of users.
The user classes shall include 'data consumers', 'data and database managers', and 'network information infrastructure resource managers'.
'On-site personnel' must be able to tune the data management services.
The ability to tune data management services must be available when the system is in a 'fixed (static) mode of operation'.
The ability to tune data management services must be available at a 'base or data processing megacenter'.
Tuning data management services must include the ability to 'adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases'.
The qualification methods applied to the software must include all of the approaches: test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection.
Personnel must have the ability to redefine or reset the names of connect descriptors to database server instances if a server name or location changes.
The ability to redefine or reset the names of connect descriptors to database server instances must be transparent to the operator.
The system must internally interface with the existing data elements of the DBIF.
The system must internally interface with the existing data elements of the DAC.
The system must internally interface with the existing data elements of COTS RDBMS products.
As a general rule, all XS components for the DII COE must adhere to the compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.
Training requirements for the XS module are to be determined by the developers of the main system.
The system must support bringing parts of the system online or offline.
The system must support being managed in specific states like 'open not-mounted'.
The session time-out values must be user definable.
Session time-out values must be able to be reset before a user session is initialized.
The Registry software is required to execute on any COE-compliant platforms.
If a user cannot find an existing specification ('spec') in the Registry to reuse for their task, then they must create a new one.
The system must provide verification methods to confirm that adjustments are correct and have not introduced any issues.
Maintenance activities shall cover planned shutdowns, upgrades, and modifications.
